signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: NZD is the weakest G10 currency this week, with NZD 1W avg -1.492% (n=4 pairs) vs USD 1W avg +0.608% (n=7 pairs), creating a 2.100% spread. This strong divergence is amplified by NZD/USD COT LF net short −22,454 (long%=18.4%), an extreme speculative short position against an RBNZ with an explicit 'Hike' bias for its next meeting on 8 Jul. The options market shows extreme retail long (94% long) which is likely to be squeezed if the RBNZ delivers. NZD/USD at 0.5657. Watch for RBNZ rhetoric on 8 Jul to confirm short-squeeze potential.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Elevated Vol Premium
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.55, trading above the 160.00 level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. The 264bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.62% vs TONA 0.98%) continues to support the pair, despite a crowded JPY COT LF net short −112,092 (long%=29.7%). Options market pricing elevated protection with USD/JPY IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.3% (VRP +4.9%), indicating strong hedging demand for JPY weakness. The 25d RR at -1.20 shows moderate put skew, suggesting options traders are hedging against further JPY depreciation. Intervention risk rises on sustained price action above 160.00, especially with the next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Outlook + COT Positioning
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1413. Eurozone consumer one-year inflation expectations cooled to 3.5% (ECB survey), yet the ECB is still perceived to be on a hiking path for September, as per recent headlines. This contrasts with EUR COT LF net short −40,372 (long%=39.1%), indicating speculative shorts are fading a potentially hawkish ECB. The 144bp USD carry premium (SOFR 3.62% vs €STR 2.18%) structurally limits EUR/USD upside. Elevated EUR/USD IV 7.1% vs HV30 5.0% (VRP +2.1%) suggests hedging demand for EUR downside. Watch for ECB commentary ahead of the 23 Jul meeting for clarity on the hiking path.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Complacency + Negative CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6906. The pair exhibits unusual options market complacency with IV 3.1% significantly below HV30 8.2%, resulting in a negative VRP of -5.0%. This suggests options traders are not pricing in significant future volatility despite AUD's recent weakness (1W: -1.53%, 1M: -3.51%). The AUD economic surprise index is moderately negative (net -7 misses), indicating data is consistently disappointing expectations, which supports AUD weakness. The 73bp AUD carry premium (AONIA 4.35% vs SOFR 3.62%) is not enough to offset the negative sentiment. Watch for a shift in economic data surprises to invalidate the current complacency.
